mission.title: Install and Configure Open WebUI
mission.description: Open WebUI is a user-friendly AI interface designed for self-hosting, supporting various LLM runners like Ollama and OpenAI APIs. It offers seamless integration and extensibility, making it ideal for developers looking to deploy AI solutions quickly and efficiently.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the necessary command-line tools for managing your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

curl -LO https://storage.googleapis.com/kubernetes-release/release/$(curl -s https://storage.googleapis.com/kubernetes-release/release/stable.txt)/bin/linux/amd64/kubectl

chmod +x ./kubectl

sudo mv ./kubectl /usr/local/bin/kubectl

```

> Download the latest stable version of kubectl.
> Make the kubectl binary executable.
> Move kubectl to a directory in your PATH.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Set up a new Kubernetes cluster on Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create open-webui-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Create a GKE cluster with 3 nodes in the specified region.

steps.title: Set Up kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure kubectl to use the new cluster's credentials.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials open-webui-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Fetch the cluster credentials and configure kubectl.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check the status of the nodes in the cluster to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes in the cluster and their statuses.

steps.title: Install Open WebUI
steps.description:
Deploy Open WebUI using Helm with the specified version.

```bash

helm repo add open-webui https://charts.openwebui.com

helm install open-webui open-webui/open-webui --version 0.8.8 --namespace open-webui --create-namespace

```

> Add the Open WebUI Helm repository.
> Install Open WebUI in the specified namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Set up autoscaling and monitoring for the Open WebUI deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment open-webui --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=5 --namespace open-webui

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/open-webui/open-webui/main/monitoring.yaml --namespace open-webui

```

> Enable autoscaling for the Open WebUI deployment.
> Deploy monitoring configuration for Open WebUI.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Open WebUI
uninstall.description:
Remove Open WebUI and all associated resources. This will result in data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

helm uninstall open-webui --namespace open-webui

gcloud container clusters delete open-webui-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> Uninstall the Open WebUI Helm release.
> Delete the GKE cluster and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Open WebUI
upgrade.description:
Backup existing configurations, upgrade the deployment, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get all --namespace open-webui -o yaml > open-webui-backup.yaml

helm upgrade open-webui open-webui/open-webui --version 0.8.8 --namespace open-webui

kubectl get pods --namespace open-webui

```

> Backup current Open WebUI configurations.
> Upgrade Open WebUI to the latest version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get all --namespace open-webui -o yaml > open-webui-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error from server (NotFound): deployments.apps "open-webui" not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Open WebUI deployment may not have been created successfully.

**Fix:**
Check the Helm installation logs and ensure the deployment was created correctly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: unable to connect to the server: dial tcp <IP>:<PORT>: i/o timeout'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes cluster may not be reachable or is not running.

**Fix:**
Verify the cluster status using 'gcloud container clusters list' and ensure it is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: failed to pull image "openwebui/open-webui:0.8.8": rpc error: code = Unknown desc = Error response from daemon: pull access denied'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The image may not be available or the repository is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Ensure the image name and tag are correct and accessible.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: unable to recognize "<file>": no matches for kind "Deployment" in version "apps/v1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes API version may not support the specified resource kind.

**Fix:**
Check the Kubernetes version compatibility and ensure the correct API version is used.

**Affected versions:** 1.26, 1.27, 1.28, 1.29, 1.30

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new LLM runners and improved user interface features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods --namespace open-webui' showing all pods in the 'Running' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
